title: DSDS Link Definition
description: A typed reference to an external resource, a related artifact, or an internal DSDS entity. Used across all DSDS document types for source code, design files, documentation, packages, artifact relationships, and internal cross-references. Links unify what was previously split between the `links` array (external URLs) and the `artifact-reference` guideline type (internal entity references) into a single relationship model. Every link requires a `kind` and at least one of `url` (for external resources) or `name` (for internal DSDS artifact references). Both can be present when an internal artifact also has an addressable URL.

link.description: A typed reference to an external resource or an internal DSDS artifact. Links serve three purposes: pointing to external resources (source code, design files, documentation, packages), expressing relationships to other DSDS artifacts by name (components in a pattern, token groups in a style), and combining both when an internal artifact also has an addressable URL. The `kind` property classifies the reference. Standard external types: 'source', 'design', 'storybook', 'documentation', 'package', 'repository'. Standard relationship types: 'alternative', 'parent', 'child', 'related'. Standard artifact types: 'component', 'token', 'token-group', 'style', 'pattern', 'theme'. Custom values are permitted.

kind.description: The category of the linked resource or relationship. Standard external types: 'source', 'design', 'storybook', 'documentation', 'package', 'repository'. Standard relationship types: 'alternative', 'parent', 'child', 'related'. Standard artifact types (for internal references): 'component', 'token', 'token-group', 'style', 'pattern', 'theme'. Custom values are permitted and SHOULD be lowercase strings matching ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$.

url.description: The URL of the linked resource. MUST be a valid absolute URI. Required for external resource links. Optional for internal artifact references (where `name` identifies the artifact). When both `url` and `name` are present, `url` points to the artifact's documentation page or canonical location while `name` provides the machine-readable cross-reference.

name.description: The name of a referenced DSDS artifact (e.g., 'button', 'form-field', 'color-text', 'spacing'). MUST match the `name` property of a documented DSDS artifact. Required for internal artifact references. Optional for external resource links. When present, tools SHOULD resolve this name against the artifact catalog to create navigable cross-references.

label.description: The display text for this link — what a user sees when the link is rendered in documentation (e.g., 'React component source', 'Design file — variants', 'Button component'). Use label for the human-readable name of the resource. For describing the functional relationship an internal artifact has to the current entity, use `role` instead.

role.description: The functional relationship this artifact has to the current entity — what part it plays in the current context (e.g., 'Primary action trigger', 'Error summary container', 'Provides semantic text color tokens'). Distinct from `label`, which is display text for the link itself. Use role for internal artifact references to explain *why* this artifact is referenced, not just *what* it is. When omitted, the relationship is a general association without a specific role.

required.description: Whether this linked artifact is required for the parent entity to function correctly. Primarily used for internal artifact references — for example, a form-validation pattern might mark 'form-field' as required and 'toast' as optional. When false or omitted, the artifact is an optional enhancement or supplementary reference. Not applicable to external resource links.
